LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT W.C. DRILLING INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH/BARROWS MOTION. NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT PARTNERS CONTRACT, INC. HAS A REASONABLE EXCUSE FOR DELAY - IT LEARNED ONLY IN DISCOVERY OF W.C. DRILLING'S ROLE IN CAUSING THE ALLEGED DAMAGE. (SEE CCP 474; MCOWEN V. GROSSMAN (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 937, 942.) MOREOVER, ANY PREJUDICE IS LIMITED; W.C. HAS KNOWN IT WOULD BE SUED SINCE AT LEAST JANUARY, WHICH WAS NINE MONTHS BEFORE THE CURRENTLY PENDING TRIAL DATE. MOTIONS FOR TRIAL CONTINUANCE ARE HEARD ONLY IN DEPARTMENT 206. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM TO THE COURT'S RULING AND STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. Motion To Quash Barrows Motion And Declaration Of Ramsey Kubein
Off Calendar May-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT W.C. DRILLING INC. DBA WEST COAST DRILLING's Motion To Quash Barrows Motion And Declaration Of Ramsey Kubein

Cross-defendant W.C. Drilling, Inc.'s motion to quash/Barrows motion is DENIED. Cross-complainant Partners Contract, Inc. has a reasonable excuse for delay - it learned only in discovery of W.C. Drilling's role in causing the alleged damage. (See CCP 474; McOwen v. Grossman (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 937, 942.) Moreover, any prejudice is limited; W.C. has known it would be sued since at least January, which was nine months before the currently pending trial date. Motions for trial continuance are heard only in Department 206.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order that is verbatim to the Court's ruling and states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
